WELCOME.TAGLINE: A fast, Git-friendly, and open-source API client.
WELCOME.FILESYSTEM_DESC: Collections are plain files on your disk. No cloud sync, no proprietary lock-in.
WELCOME.GIT_FRIENDLY_DESC: Every request is a readable file. Commit, branch, review, and collaborate using the tools you already know.
WELCOME.PRIVACY_FOCUSED_DESC: No account, no login. Bruno works entirely offline, your API keys never leave your machine.
WELCOME.FAST_LIGHTWEIGHT_DESC: Built to be snappy. No bloated runtimes, just a fast, focused tool for exploring and testing APIs.
WELCOME.ALL_SET_DESC: Create a new collection to start building requests, or import one you already have.
WELCOME.CREATE_COLLECTION_DESC: Start fresh with a new API collection
WELCOME.IMPORT_COLLECTION_DESC: Bring in Postman, OpenAPI/Swagger, or Insomnia
WELCOME.OPEN_EXISTING_DESC: Open a Bruno collection from your filesystem
WELCOME.START_REQUEST_DESC: Jump right in with a new HTTP request
WELCOME.CHOOSE_THEME_DESC: Pick a look that feels right. You can always change this later in Preferences.
WELCOME.STORAGE_TITLE: Where should we store your collections?
WELCOME.STORAGE_DESC: Bruno saves collections as plain files on your filesystem, perfect for version control with Git.
WELCOME.STORAGE_HINT: Each collection and workspace gets its own folder inside this directory. You can change this later.

PREFERENCES.SAVE_DELAY_REQUIRED: Save Delay is required when Auto Save is enabled

PREFERENCES.SHORTCUT_MAC_MODIFIER: macOS shortcuts must include at least one modifier (command/alt/shift/ctrl).
PREFERENCES.SHORTCUT_WINDOWS_MODIFIER: Windows shortcuts must include at least one modifier (ctrl/alt/shift).

PREFERENCES.SHORTCUT_RESERVED: This shortcut is reserved by the OS.
PREFERENCES.SHORTCUT_IN_USE: This shortcut is already in use.
PREFERENCES.SHORTCUTS_RESET_SUCCESS: All shortcuts have been reset to default

COLLECTION_DOCS.TITLE: Documentation
COLLECTION_DOCS.PLACEHOLDER:
Welcome to your collection documentation! This space is designed to help you document your API collection effectively.

## Overview
Use this section to provide a high-level overview of your collection. You can describe:
- The purpose of these API endpoints
- Key features and functionalities
- Target audience or users

## Best Practices
- Keep documentation up to date
- Include request/response examples
- Document error scenarios
- Add relevant links and references

## Markdown Support
This documentation supports Markdown formatting! You can use:
- **Bold** and *italic* text
- `code blocks` and syntax highlighting
- Tables and lists
- [Links](https://usebruno.com)
- And more!

FOLDER_SETTINGS.EXPR_INFO: You can write any valid JS expression here
FOLDER_SETTINGS.HEADERS_DESCRIPTION: Request headers that will be sent with every request inside this folder.
FOLDER_SETTINGS.SCRIPT_DESCRIPTION: Pre and post-request scripts that will run before and after any request inside this folder is sent.
FOLDER_SETTINGS.TESTS_DESCRIPTION: These tests will run any time a request in this collection is sent.
FOLDER_SETTINGS.AUTH_DESCRIPTION: Configures authentication for the entire folder. This applies to all requests using the Inherit option in the Auth tab.

OPENAPI_SYNC.BETA_FEEDBACK: OpenAPI Sync is in Beta — we'd love to hear your feedback and suggestions.
OPENAPI_SYNC.CONNECT_DESCRIPTION: Keep your collection synchronized with an OpenAPI specification. Changes in the spec will be detected automatically.
OPENAPI_SYNC.SUPPORTS_OPENAPI: Supports OpenAPI 3.x specifications in JSON or YAML format
OPENAPI_SYNC.SELECT_LOCAL_FILE: Select a local OpenAPI/Swagger JSON or YAML file
OPENAPI_SYNC.FEATURE_DETECT_ENDPOINTS: Detect new, modified, and removed endpoints
OPENAPI_SYNC.FEATURE_TRACK_CHANGES: Track local changes against the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.FEATURE_SYNC_COLLECTION: Sync collection with a single click
OPENAPI_SYNC.FEATURE_PRESERVE_TESTS: Your tests, assertions, and scripts are preserved during sync
OPENAPI_SYNC.ERROR_INVALID_SPEC: The selected file is not a valid OpenAPI 3.x specification
OPENAPI_SYNC.ERROR_SWAGGER_NOT_SUPPORTED: Swagger 2.0 is not supported. Please convert your spec to OpenAPI 3.x.
OPENAPI_SYNC.ERROR_READ_FILE: Failed to read the selected file
OPENAPI_SYNC.ERROR_ENTER_URL_OR_FILE: Please enter a URL or select a file
OPENAPI_SYNC.ERROR_INVALID_URL_SPEC: The URL does not point to a valid OpenAPI 3.x specification
OPENAPI_SYNC.COLLECTION_UP_TO_DATE: Collection is up to date with the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.COLLECTION_NOT_UP_TO_DATE: Collection is not up to date with the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.REVEAL_FAILED: Failed to open in file manager
OPENAPI_SYNC.IN_SYNC_WITH_SPEC_TOOLTIP: Endpoints that currently match the latest spec from the source
OPENAPI_SYNC.CHANGED_IN_COLLECTION_TOOLTIP: Endpoints modified, deleted, or added locally since last sync
OPENAPI_SYNC.SPEC_UPDATES_PENDING_TOOLTIP: Spec changes available to sync to your collection
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_FAILED_CHECK: Failed to check for spec updates
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_INITIAL_SYNC_REQUIRED: Initial sync required — your collection differs from the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_INITIAL_SYNC_REQUIRED_DESC: Review the changes and sync to bring your collection up to date.
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_SPEC_AND_COLLECTION_CHANGES: OpenAPI spec has new updates{{versionInfo}} and the collection has changes
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_SPEC_AND_COLLECTION_CHANGES_DESC: New or changed requests are available. Some collection changes may be overwritten.
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_SPEC_HAS_UPDATES_DESC: New or changed requests are available.
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_SPEC_NOT_FOUND_DESC: The last synced spec is missing in the storage. Restore the latest spec from the source to track collection changes.
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_COLLECTION_HAS_CHANGES: Collection has changes not in the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.BANNER_COLLECTION_HAS_CHANGES_DESC: Some requests have been modified or removed and no longer match the spec.
OPENAPI_SYNC.COLLECTION_HAS_CHANGES_SINCE_LAST_SYNC: Collection has changes since last sync
OPENAPI_SYNC.WHATS_TRACKED_DESC: Changes to parameters, headers, body and auth compared to the synced spec. Your variables, scripts, tests, assertions, settings etc. are not tracked here.
OPENAPI_SYNC.RESET_ALL_MODIFIED_DESC: Reset all modified endpoints to match the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.RESTORE_ALL_DELETED_DESC: Add all deleted endpoints back to collection
OPENAPI_SYNC.COMPARING_COLLECTION_WITH_SPEC: Comparing your collection with the last synced spec...
OPENAPI_SYNC.CANNOT_TRACK_CHANGES_DESC: The last synced spec is missing. Go to the 'Spec Updates' tab to restore it, or sync the collection if updates are available to track future changes.
OPENAPI_SYNC.WAITING_FOR_INITIAL_SYNC_DESC: Once you sync your collection with the spec, local changes will appear here.
OPENAPI_SYNC.NO_CHANGES_IN_COLLECTION_DESC: The collection endpoints match the last synced spec. Nothing to review.
OPENAPI_SYNC.FIX_CONNECTION_ISSUE: Fix the connection issue above and check again.
OPENAPI_SYNC.NO_UPDATES_FROM_SPEC_DESC: The spec endpoints have not been updated since the last sync. You can restore the spec file to track local collection changes.
OPENAPI_SYNC.CONFIRM_SYNC_NO_CHANGES_DESC: Your collection is already in sync with the remote spec. Syncing will update the local spec file to match the latest remote version.
OPENAPI_SYNC.CONFIRM_SYNC_DESC: The following changes will be applied to your collection. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure you want to proceed?
OPENAPI_SYNC.DISCONNECT_SYNC_CONFIRM: Are you sure you want to disconnect OpenAPI sync?
OPENAPI_SYNC.DISCONNECT_SYNC_CONFIRM_DESC: This will only disconnect the sync configuration. Your collection will remain intact.
OPENAPI_SYNC.AUTO_CHECK_FOR_UPDATES_DESC: Automatically check for spec changes at a regular interval
OPENAPI_SYNC.SPEC_DIFF_MISSING_SPEC_DESC: The current spec file is missing. The full remote spec is shown below.
OPENAPI_SYNC.SPEC_DIFF_DESC: Side-by-side diff of your current spec vs the updated spec from the spec URL.
OPENAPI_SYNC.DIFF_RENDER_ERROR: Diff couldn't be rendered. Please file an issue with the spec.
OPENAPI_SYNC.CONFLICT_HELP: This endpoint was modified in both the spec and your collection. Choose which version to keep.
OPENAPI_SYNC.CONFLICT_SECTION_HELP: {{count}} conflicted endpoint requires review before sync.
OPENAPI_SYNC.REVIEW_CHANGES_DESC: Choose to keep the current version or accept the updated one.
OPENAPI_SYNC.CHECKING_FOR_UPDATES_DESC: Comparing your last synced spec with the latest spec...
OPENAPI_SYNC.NO_UPDATES_FROM_SPEC_DESC_SHORT: The spec endpoints have not been updated since the last sync.
OPENAPI_SYNC.UPDATED_IN_SPEC_DESC: The spec has updates for these endpoints
OPENAPI_SYNC.REMOVED_FROM_SPEC_DESC: These endpoints are in your collection but not in the spec
OPENAPI_SYNC.WHAT_GETS_UPDATED_DESC: Parameters, headers, body and auth will be updated. Tests, scripts, and assertions are always preserved.
OPENAPI_SYNC.SYNC_WILL_UPDATE_SPEC: Sync will update the spec file
OPENAPI_SYNC.RESET_ALL_MODIFIED_CONFIRM: Reset all {{count}} modified endpoints to the spec?
OPENAPI_SYNC.REVERT_ALL_CHANGES_CONFIRM: Revert all collection changes? This will reset {{modifiedCount}} modified, restore {{missingCount}} missing, and delete {{localOnlyCount}} local endpoints.
OPENAPI_SYNC.RESTORE_ALL_MISSING_CONFIRM: Restore all {{count}} deleted endpoints to the collection?

REQUEST_AUTH.INHERITED_AUTH_NOT_SUPPORTED_GRPC: Inherited auth not supported by gRPC. Using no auth instead.
REQUEST_AUTH.INHERITED_AUTH_NOT_SUPPORTED_WS: Inherited auth not supported by WebSockets. Using no auth instead.
REQUEST_AUTH.OAUTH2_NOT_SUPPORTED_WS: OAuth 2 not yet supported by WebSockets. Using no auth instead.

QUERY_BUILDER.NO_SCHEMA_DESCRIPTION: Load a GraphQL schema to explore operations and build queries visually.
QUERY_BUILDER.MULTIPLE_OPERATIONS_DESCRIPTION: The Query Builder supports a single operation at a time. Combine into one operation to sync.
QUERY_BUILDER.ERROR_DESCRIPTION: The Query Builder encountered an unexpected error. Try reloading the schema or manually using the editor.
QUERY_BUILDER.LIST_COMPLEX_UNSUPPORTED: List arguments for complex types are not currently supported.

SECURITY_SETTINGS.SAFE_MODE_DESC: JavaScript code is executed in a secure sandbox and cannot access your filesystem or execute system commands.
SECURITY_SETTINGS.DEVELOPER_MODE_DESC: JavaScript code has access to the filesystem, can execute system commands and access sensitive information.
SECURITY_SETTINGS.DEVELOPER_MODE_WARNING: Use only if you trust the authors of the collection

CREATE_UNTITLED_REQUEST.ERROR_ADDING_REQUEST: An error occurred while adding the request

REQUEST_SETTINGS.MAX_REDIRECTS_DESC: Set a limit for the number of redirects to follow
REQUEST_SETTINGS.TIMEOUT_DESC: Set maximum time to wait before aborting the request

RESPONSE_PANE.STREAMING_NOT_SUPPORTED: Response Examples aren't supported in streaming responses yet.
RESPONSE_PANE.SIZE_EXCEEDS_LIMIT: Response size exceeds 5MB limit. Cannot save as example.

RUNNER_RESULTS.CLICK_STATUS_CODE: Click status code to see response

CREATE_DIALOG.TITLE: Create Workspace
CREATE_DIALOG.DESCRIPTION: Give your new workspace a name and choose its location to get started.
CREATE_DIALOG.WORKSPACE_ALREADY_EXISTS: A workspace with this name already exists
CREATE_DIALOG.FOLDER_NAME_HELP_1: The name of the folder used to store the workspace.
CREATE_DIALOG.FOLDER_NAME_HELP_2: You can choose a folder name different from your workspace name or one compatible with filesystem rules.
CREATE_DIALOG.LOCATION_HELP_1: Bruno stores your workspaces on your computer's filesystem.
CREATE_DIALOG.LOCATION_HELP_2: Choose the location where you want to store this workspace.

IMPORT_DIALOG.DROP_FILE_PREFIX: Drop workspace zip file here or
